value.rationale: Hooks is an extensibility/policy framework embedded in OpenAI Codex's agentic loop that fires user-defined scripts at lifecycle events (PreToolUse, PermissionRequest, PostToolUse, etc.). Within guardrails-safety it covers only a slice — it is a hook/interception mechanism enabling custom policy enforcement, validation, and blocking at tool boundaries, but it is not a full guardrails system (no built-in classifiers, no output filtering, no RBAC). It also lives squarely within agentic-coding (Codex CLI). It is a feature/sub-product of openai-codex, configured via hooks.json or config.toml and enabled by default.
